cowardice_flags.avoided: what specific personal incentives (cabinet posts, GLC control, titles, patronage networks) the 2016 defectors from UMNO actually received or negotiated as immediate payoff
cowardice_flags.should_say: The successful Bersatu launch hinged not just on seats/grievance/slot but on explicit personal material rewards for key defectors, mirroring the same elite self-interest that enabled the 2020 Sheraton Move

cowardice_flags.avoided: the full weight of the 2014 Sabah RCI findings on Project IC-style voter manipulation via illegal IC grants
cowardice_flags.should_say: Sabah's eight configurations in 40 years were not random cleavage volatility but were structurally enabled by deliberate citizenship engineering documented by the official RCI

cowardice_flags.avoided: the real costs of GPS 'stability' — systematic opposition marginalisation, media dominance, and suppression of dissent
cowardice_flags.should_say: Sarawak's RM2.7–4bn annual SST revenue buys structural stability precisely because GPS controls patronage, delineation, and narrative in ways that make genuine opposition nearly impossible

cowardice_flags.avoided: that for most B40 voters (especially rural Malay/Iban/Orang Asli reliant on JKKK appointments and handouts), judging 'who will be in the room' is not empowering bargaining but a survival calculation with no real alternative
cowardice_flags.should_say: The 'room' is closed to B40 voters except as clients of the patronage machine; JKKK appointments and targeted aid make opposition voting economically suicidal for village heads and their communities

audience_check: A taxi driver in Ipoh or factory worker in Johor would eye-roll at the abstract Shapley-Shubik and Lipset-Rokkan theory — it feels like elite lecture on why their vote doesn't matter. The B40 Malay village head reliant on JKKK would feel spoken past. The young Iban graduate in Kuching or Orang Asli activist would tune out the Sarawak section. Only urban middle-class readers share this on WhatsApp; ground-level Malaysians would call it sophisticated cynicism dressed as analysis.
hard_truths_missing: NEP created broad Malay dependency and elite capture more than broad-based uplift.
Chinese education system survives via heavy self-financing because of political marginalisation.
Indian community has highest relative marginalisation (~40% in B40, lowest home ownership).
Every major coalition runs on patronage; 1MDB-scale corruption is systemic not unique to one party.
Article 40 discretion creates legitimacy crises — unelected monarch can override parliamentary numbers.
PAS is a veto player on social policy, not symmetric to other cleavages.
Civil servants are a locked-in patronage bloc.

predictions.if_nothing_changes: Another round of elite defections will produce a hung parliament after GE16/17; new vanity parties will launch and pivot for posts.
Sabah/Sarawak kingmaker role intensifies; federal coalitions buy state stability with fiscal concessions while B40 voters remain locked out.

strongest_section.why: Applies Lipset-Rokkan rigorously without obvious hedging

weakest_section.why: Voter-as-bargaining-chip metaphor collapses when confronted with B40 patronage reality

overall_assessment: The dossier delivers a clean, theoretically literate framework on the launch-and-pivot machine but flinches every time elite self-interest, personal payoffs, Sabah/Sarawak ground realities, or B40 powerlessness would make the analysis truly uncomfortable. It lectures the taxi driver and village head in elegant abstractions while soft-pedaling the raw patronage, ethnic undercurrents, and democratic-legitimacy costs. Courageous on structure, cowardly on flesh-and-blood incentives.
